title: हनुमान अष्टोत्तर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान अष्टोत्तरशतनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

श्री हनुमान् आञ्जनेयश्च महावीरो महाबलः।
रामेष्टः फाल्गुनसखः पिङ्गाक्षो अमितविक्रमः॥१॥

उदधिक्रमणश्चैव सीताशोकविनाशनः।
लक्ष्मणप्राणदाताच दशग्रीवस्य दर्पहा॥२॥

वायुपुत्रो अमिताभश्च चतुर्बाहुसमन्वितः।
द्वात्रिंशल्लक्षणोपेतः सर्वाभीष्टफलप्रदः॥३॥

सुग्रीवसचिवो धीरः शूरो दैत्यकुलान्तकः।
सुरार्चितः महातेजाः रामचूडामणिप्रदः॥४॥

कामरूपी पिङ्गलाक्षो वार्धिमथनोद्यतः।
कबलीकृतमार्ताण्ड मण्डलो विजितेन्द्रियः॥५॥

रामसुग्रीवसन्धाता महारावणमर्दनः।
स्फटिकाभो वागधीशो नवव्याकृतिपण्डितः॥६॥

चतुर्बाहुर्दीनबन्धुर्महात्मा भक्तवत्सलः।
संजीवनगिरिहर्ता शुचिर्वाग्मी दृढव्रतः॥७॥

कालनेमिप्रमथनः हरिमर्कटमर्कटः।
दान्तो शान्तः प्रसन्नात्मा शतकण्ठमदापहः॥८॥

योगी रामकथालोलः सीतान्वेषणपण्डितः।
वज्रदंष्ट्रो वज्रनखो रुद्रवीर्यसमुद्भवः॥९॥

इन्द्रजित्प्रहितामोघ ब्रह्मास्त्रविनिवारकः।
पार्थध्वजसमारूढो शरपञ्जरभेदकः॥१०॥

दशबाहुर्लोकपूज्यो जाम्बवत्प्रीतिवर्धनः।
सीतासमेतश्रीरामपादसेवाधुरन्धरः॥११॥

॥ इति श्री हनुमान अष्टोत्तरशतनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Ashtottarashatanama Stotram ||

Shri Hanuman Aanjaneyashcha Mahaviiro Mahabalah.
Raameshthah Phaalguna-Sakhah Pingaaksho Amitavikramah.||1||

Udadhikramanashchaiva Sitaashoka-Vinaashanah.
Lakshmana-Praana-Daatacha Dashagreevasya Darpahaa.||2||

Vaayuputro Amitaabhashcha Chaturbaahusamanvitah.
Dvaatrimshal-Lakshanoopetah Sarvaabhishta-Phalapradah.||3||

Sugreeva-Sachivo Dheerah Shooro Daityakulaantakah.
Suraarchitah Mahaatejaah Ramachoodaamani-Pradah.||4||

Kaamaroopi Pingalaaksho Vaardhimathanodyatah.
Kabalikrita-Maartanda-Mandalo Vijitendriyah.||5||

Raama-Sugreeva-Sandhaata Mahaaraavana-Mardanah.
Sphatikabho Vaagadheesho Nava-Vyaakriti-Panditah.||6||

Chaturbahur-Deenabandhu-Mahaatmaa Bhaktavatsalah.
Sanjeevana-Giri-Hartaa Shuchir-Vaagmee Dridha-Vratah.||7||

Kaalanemi-Pramathanah Hari-Markata-Markatah.
Daanto Shaantah Prasannaatmaa Shatakantha-Madaapahah.||8||

Yogi Raama-Kathaalolah Seetaanveshana-Panditah.
Vajra-Damshtro Vajra-Nakho Rudra-Veerya-Samudbhavah.||9||

Indrajit-Prahitaamogha Brahmastra-Vinivarakah.
Paartha-Dhvaja-Samaarudhah Shara-Panjara-Bhedakah.||10||

Dashabaahur-Loka-Pujyo Jaambavat-Preeti-Vardhanah.
Seetaa-Sameta-Shri-Raama-Paada-Sevaa-Dhurandharah.||11||

|| Iti Shri Hanuman Ashtottarashatanama Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: यह स्तोत्र श्री हनुमान जी के 108 नामों का श्लोकबद्ध स्तवन है। इसमें हनुमान जी को आञ्जनेय (अंजनी पुत्र), महावीर, महाबल, वायुपुत्र, संजीवनगिरिहर्ता, कामरूपी, विजितेन्द्रिय, भक्तवत्सल आदि नामों से स्तुति की गई है। प्रत्येक नाम उनके किसी दिव्य गुण, पराक्रम या लीला का वर्णन करता है।
benefits: Reciting all 108 names of Hanuman grants immense spiritual strength
Traditionally recited for removal of fear, anxiety, and all forms of negativity
Invokes the blessing of courage, wisdom, and unwavering devotion to lord rama
Traditionally invoked for protection from evil spirits, black magic, and planetary afflictions
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires when chanted with sincere devotion
description: The Hanuman Ashtottara Stotram, known in Sanskrit as हनुमान अष्टोत्तर स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Hanuman. Attributed to Parasara Samhita, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to protect from evil spirits, black magic, and planetary afflictions as well as fulfill all desires when chanted with sincere devotion. Additionally, it is known to remove fear, anxiety, and all forms of negativity. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 11 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Ashtottara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Ashtottara Stotram - 108 names of Lord Hanuman in verse form with Devanagari, transliteration, meaning and benefits.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hanuman Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Ashtottara Shatanamavali is a sacred litany of 108 divine names of Lord Hanuman, also called Anjaneya, Maruti, or Bajrang Bali. Each name in the namavali highlights a specific virtue, exploit, or attribute of Hanuman, such as Anjaneya (son of Anjana), Mahavira (the great hero), Ramadasa (servant of Rama), or Vajrakaya (one with a thunderbolt body). It is recited as a complete cycle of devotion, often using a 108-bead japa mala, and is preserved across temple worship traditions in both North and South India. (Source: Drik Panchang; Hindu Devotional Blog.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are exactly 108 names used in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The number 108 holds deep ritual importance across Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain traditions. Japa malas contain 108 beads, many namavalis enumerate 108 names, and the number is associated with cosmic wholeness, the 27 nakshatras multiplied by 4 padas, and the 12 zodiac signs multiplied by 9 grahas. Reciting 108 names is considered a complete ritual cycle, balancing the practitioner's mind, breath, and devotion. The Ashtottara Shatanamavali format is one of the most common namavali templates and exists for nearly every major Hindu deity. (Source: Drik Panchang; Rudraksha Ratna.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best day to recite the Hanuman Ashtottara?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday (Mangalwar) and Saturday (Shanivar) are traditionally regarded as the most auspicious days to recite Hanuman stotras, including the Ashtottara Shatanamavali. Many devotees also chant it on Hanuman Jayanti, during the Hindu month of Chaitra, and through the 40-day Mangalwar Vrat. Daily recitation at sunrise after a bath, facing east, with a lit ghee lamp before a Hanuman image, is the prescribed temple practice. (Source: Hinduism Outlook; Chamundi Agarbathi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Ashtottara and the Hanuman Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Chalisa is a 40-verse devotional poem in Awadhi composed by Tulsidas in the 16th century, narrating Hanuman's life and virtues in continuous verse. The Hanuman Ashtottara Shatanamavali, by contrast, is a Sanskrit namavali, a structured list of 108 individual names, each typically prefixed with 'Om' and suffixed with 'namah' for ritual offering. The Chalisa is sung as a hymn; the Ashtottara is chanted name-by-name during archana (flower offering) or japa. Both are considered complementary devotional practices. (Source: Hindu Mirror; Rudraksha Ratna.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do devotees seek from this namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources state that disciplined recitation of the Hanuman Ashtottara Shatanamavali grants courage, removes fear, dispels grahabadha (planetary afflictions, especially Saturn and Mars), strengthens devotion to Sri Rama, and protects against negative influences. Each of the 108 names invokes a specific aspect of Hanuman's power, including Sankatamochana (remover of distress), Bhaktavatsala (loving to devotees), and Lakshmanapranadata (giver of life to Lakshmana). The practice is meant to cultivate inner strength rather than secure material gain alone. (Source: Hinduism Outlook; Webdunia.)
